symptoms.symptom_name: Absolute constipation — no bowel motion for four days

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Prolonged absolute constipation with distension is a hallmark of impaction.
Absolute constipation can also signal mechanical obstruction and must be excluded in person.

symptoms.symptom_name: Hard, dry, pellet-like stools with painful straining and incomplete evacuation

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Type 1 hard stools and tenesmus are typical of evolving impaction.

symptoms.symptom_name: Sensation of a hard anorectal blockage; failed digital manoeuvres

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A palpable/sensed low rectal bolus that cannot be evacuated points to a distal rectal impaction.

symptoms.symptom_name: Lower abdominal distension, bloating, poor appetite and early satiety

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Colonic loading produces distension and early satiety.
Distension is non-specific and also occurs in obstruction.

symptoms.symptom_name: Vagal near-syncope during straining with near-fall

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Straining-induced Valsalva provokes a vagal pre-syncopal response; a red flag for fall/collapse risk rather than for the diagnosis itself.
Absence of vomiting reduces (but does not exclude) the likelihood of high-grade mechanical obstruction.

symptoms.symptom_name: No PR bleeding and no fever (pertinent negatives)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of fever argues against acute diverticulitis.
Absence of PR bleeding lowers concern for an obstructing malignancy but does not exclude it.

symptoms.symptom_name: Recent codeine initiation without concurrent laxative

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Opioid-induced constipation is the reversible driver; recent codeine without laxative cover is the key aetiological clue.

symptoms.symptom_name: Rectal vault packed with rock-hard faeces on digital rectal examination

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A DRE demonstrating an impacted rectal vault is the confirmatory finding — obtainable only in person, sealed from the telehealth AI.

symptoms.symptom_name: Firm, loaded descending and sigmoid colon with voluntary guarding, no rebound/rigidity; active bowel sounds

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Palpable loaded colon without peritonism supports impaction and argues against current perforation — a clinician-only examination finding.

symptoms.symptom_name: Colonic faecal loading on plain abdominal X-ray

investigation_details.expected_result: Colonic faecal loading; no dilated obstructed bowel loops
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: A mechanical obstruction could be missed if imaging is not obtained when the picture is atypical.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Confirms colonic loading and helps exclude obstruction — imaging unavailable in telehealth.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Recent opioid + hard pellet stools + absolute constipation + anorectal blockage sensation together make opioid-induced impaction the leading diagnosis.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Confirms impaction and guides disimpaction — must be performed in person; the AI Doctor arranges it rather than performing or simulating it.
Documents colonic faecal loading and helps exclude mechanical obstruction when clinically indicated.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Urea, electrolytes and creatinine (in person bloods)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Assess hydration/renal function in a frail elder with reduced oral intake — obtainable only via an in-person pathology service.
